goal: Build a graduated tower stacker: a Franka Panda picks 5cm cubes from a conveyor and column-stacks 5 cubes vertically on top of a small tower base behind the robot. Each cube goes to a DISTINCT z-elevated drop position computed via compute_stack_placement(pattern='column'). Stress-tests cuRobo's vertical motion + EE_INITIAL_HEIGHT auto-clearance + per-cube drop_targets dispatch.
thoughts:
Six column-tower specifics for CP-09 (extends CP-01 + CP-08 — orientation rule, CPU dynamics, sleepThreshold, rubber-on-cube, drop_targets dict pattern all inherited):
1) Tower base = 0.10x0.10x0.05m flat platform at [0, -0.4, 0.775]. Smaller xy footprint than CP-08's pallet — column stack stays inside footprint without requiring xy precision.
2) compute_stack_placement(target='/World/TowerBase', pattern='column', n_items=5, cube_size=0.05, anchor='top') yields 5 positions all at xy=(0, -0.4), z = 0.825 + i*0.05 for i=0..4 → z ∈ {0.825, 0.875, 0.925, 0.975, 1.025}.
3) Tallest cube center at z=1.025 (top at z=1.05). Robot base at z=0.75 → reach to top cube is sqrt(0.4^2 + 0.275^2) ≈ 0.49m horizontal-equivalent at z+0.275m. Within Franka's 0.85m reach.
4) cuRobo handler's _compute_h1 was extended (this commit) to consider all DROP_TARGETS z-values. EE_INITIAL_HEIGHT auto = max(source_z, max_drop_z) + 0.20 = 1.025 + 0.20 = 1.225m. EE clears all 5 targets during transit.
5) 5 cubes on conveyor at x ∈ [-2.0, -1.7, -1.4, -1.1, -0.8], y=0.4 — all OUTSIDE robot's 0.70m reach so belt has visible work between picks.
6) Conveyor 4m wide (vs CP-01's 3m) to accommodate 5 cubes at 0.30m spacing. Same belt mechanics + materials as CP-01.
code:
# DomeLight + Ground + parent Xform + table
# Ground catches cubes that miss drop target or fall off conveyor end.
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.5, 0.5, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# Franka — same pose as CP-01
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

# Wider conveyor (4m vs 3m) for 5 cubes
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    position=[0.0, 0.4, 0.78],
    size=[4.0, 0.4, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.2, 0, 0],
)

# 5 cubes outside reach, 0.30m apart
for i, x in enumerate([-2.0, -1.7, -1.4, -1.1, -0.8]):
    path = f"/World/Cube_{i+1}"
    create_prim(prim_path=path, prim_type="Cube", position=[x, 0.4, 0.835], size=0.05)
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=path, schema_name=api)

bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=[f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(5)],
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
for path in [f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(5)]:
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=path, material_name="rubber")

# Tower base — small flat platform 0.10x0.10x0.05m
# Cube primitive default size=2; scale=[0.05, 0.05, 0.025] → 0.10x0.10x0.05m
# bbox: [-0.05, -0.45, 0.75] -> [0.05, -0.35, 0.80] (top flush at table top + 5cm)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/TowerBase", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, -0.4, 0.775], scale=[0.05, 0.05, 0.025])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/TowerBase", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# Pick sensor
add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path="/World/PickSensor", position=[0.4, 0.4, 0.835], size=[0.06, 0.06, 0.06])

# cuRobo controller with drop_targets dict — column stack at xy=(0, -0.4),
# z increases by cube_size=0.05 per cube. Computed via:
#   compute_stack_placement(target='/World/TowerBase', pattern='column',
#                            n_items=5, cube_size=0.05, anchor='top')
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    belt_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    source_paths=[f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(5)],
    destination_path="/World/TowerBase",
    drop_targets={
        "/World/Cube_1": [0.0, -0.4, 0.825],
        "/World/Cube_2": [0.0, -0.4, 0.875],
        "/World/Cube_3": [0.0, -0.4, 0.925],
        "/World/Cube_4": [0.0, -0.4, 0.975],
        "/World/Cube_5": [0.0, -0.4, 1.025],
    },
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/ConveyorBelt", "/World/TowerBase"],
)
extension_notes: CP-09 reuses CP-08's pattern (compute_stack_placement + drop_targets dict + cuRobo) but switches pattern from grid_2x2 to column. Stress-tests vertical motion: tallest drop at z=1.025m, EE_INITIAL_HEIGHT clears at 1.225m. _compute_h1 in cuRobo handler must consider all DROP_TARGETS z (extended in this commit). Future variants: Graduated Tower with decreasing-size cubes (CP-N: needs per-cube cube_size), Pinwheel layer (CP-N: needs new pattern in compute_stack_placement).
failure_modes: EE_INITIAL_HEIGHT not clearing tallest target — h1 computed only from sources or single drop_target, not all DROP_TARGETS. Verify _compute_h1 in cuRobo handler iterates DROP_TARGETS.values() (fixed in this commit).
Cube N landing on top of N-1 with collision before grip release — happens if drop z too tight to physical cube_size. Use exactly cube_size spacing (0.05m); +5mm gives bounce.

Tower instability — cubes are 5cm rubber on 5cm rubber. After 3-4 layers, slight misalignment cumulates and stack tips. CP-09 verifies form-gate only; sustained tower stability is a function-gate concern needing physics tuning.

Robot can't reach z=1.025m from base at z=0.75 — Franka's reach is ~0.85m sphere; 1.025-0.75=0.275m vertical at xy=(0,-0.4) → 0.49m total radius which is well within 0.85m. Should reach. If reach fails: lower TowerBase or scale tower height.
drop_targets list-form (instead of dict) — cube i goes to drop_targets[i] BY ORDER in source_paths, NOT by cube_path. Use dict form for robustness against source_paths reordering.
verified_status: build-verified-2026-05-07; form-gate ✓; function-gate STOCHASTIC: across 2 single-cube probes, one run reached pallet-area at rest, one returned numerical-blowup state (cube_final z<-11000m, vel >4000 m/s). Likely cuRobo seed variance + smaller TowerBase gives narrower margin for placement. Future work: lower drop precision tolerance OR larger TowerBase OR multi-run N-of-M acceptance (per T4-stochastic memory).
